response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong web, distributed-systems, and DLang background, but C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus an undergraduate thesis. The role requires 8–12 years building secure, high-performance C++ systems for industrial automation, with IEC 62443, MQTT, and industrial protocols — none of which appear on the resume. Skill set mismatch outweighs the general seniority.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering with multi-threading and high-throughput experience at Sociomantic
Comfortable across web, embedded, and distributed systems with event-driven architectures
Strong testing/TDD culture, mentoring, and public technical workshops
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~6 months in 2014–2015)
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443, SDL) or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT)
Career trajectory is JS/TS and DLang-focused, not aligned with a C++ platform role

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth for senior role
Strong general engineering seniority and multi-threaded systems exposure
